content_en:
A Description Of The People Who Will Be Alive During The End Times

‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr a related that the Messenger of Allah # said, “The Hour will not arrive until Allah takes His Shariah from the people of the earth, and the ‘Uijaajah (the mob and the common masses) will be left on it (Le. on earth) — they will neither promote good nor forbid evil.” (Ahmad) And the following was related by ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Amr -#e, who ascribed it to the Prophet 2%: “Until Allah takes His Shariah from the people.”

Indeed Some Forms Of Speech Are Magic

‘Abdullah ibn Mas‘ood < reported that he heard the Messenger of Allah #% say, “Indeed, some forms of speech are magic. And (only) the worst of people will be alive when the Hour overtakes them and those who take their graves (i.e. the graves of people from them) as Masaajid.’” (Ahmad) The chain of this narration is authentic. “Abdullah . said, “The Messenger of Allah #¢ said, ‘The Hour will not come except upon the most evil of people.” (A/mad) Muslim related the same hadith through a different chain,

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 187

Just Before The Coming Of The Hour People Will Become Bereft Of The Human Aspect

We have already mentioned the following hadith: “Men will be few, and women will be great in number, to the extent that for every 50 women, there will be a single (male) guardian, to whom they will resort to. And they will do lewd acts in the streets just as beasts do.” Since we have already mentioned the different chains and wordings of this narration, we will not do so here. And all praise is for Allah.

The Hour Will Not Descend Upon One Who Is An Islamic Monotheist

Anas reported that the Messenger of Allah #% said, “The Hour will not arrive until it is no longer said on earth, Laa ilagha illallaah (none has the right to be worshipped save Allah).” (Ahmad) Muslim related the same hadith, through different narrators, and with the following wording: “The Hour will not arrive until it is no longer said on earth, Allah, Allah.”

Anas # reported that the Messenger of Allah 2g said, “The Hour will not arrive upon one who says, ‘Allah, Allah,” (Ahmad) Muslim related the same, but through a different chain of narrators.

Anas related that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “The Hour will not arrive until it is no longer said on earth, ‘Allah, Allah.” (Alimad) This chain fulfills the conditions of both Bukhaaree and Muslim. AtTirmidhee said about his chain for this narration, “Hasan.” At- ‘Tirmidhee then related it as a saying of Anas . and then said, “This is more authentic than the first.”

The Hour Will Only Descend Upon Those Who Neither Promote Good Nor Forbid Evil

This is indicated by the saying of the Prophet #, “Until it is no longer said on earth, ‘Allah, Allah.”

‘There are two opinions regarding this:

188 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

will neither promote good nor forbid evil.”

2) Allah %@ will not be mentioned on earth, nor will His Name be known on it. And that is during an era which will be most corrupt — when human beings will become bereft of the human aspect in them, a time when there will be widespread disbelief, wickedness, and disobedience of Allah 4. And this meaning is indicated in another hadith: “The Hour will not arrive until it is no longer said on earth, Laailaaha illallaah (None has the right to be worshipped but Allah).”

The Most Evil Of People Are Alive When The Hour Comes

We already mentioned the following hadith: “Indeed the old man will say, ‘We were around when people in the past would say: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah.’ Then the matter will intensify, and the situation will worsen, until Allah’s mention will no longer be made on earth: it will be entirely forgotten, and it will not be known on earth, Those (who are around at that time) are the worst of people, and upon them the Hour will arrive.” And in another narration, the Prophet #% said, “The Hour will not arrive except upon the most wicked of people.” This is expressed slightly differently in the following narration: “The most evil of people are those who will be alive when the Hour overtakes them.”

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 189

weak, until the Hour arrives upon them.”

The Different Chains For The hadith: “] Have Been Sent And The Hour Like These Two”

‘Anas ibn Maalik .& went to Al-Waleed ibn ‘Abdul-Malik, and the latter asked of the former, “What sayings have you heard from the Messenger of Allah #% in which he #& mentioned the Hour?” He said, “I heard the Messenger of Allah 48 say, ‘You and the Hour are like these two (in other narrations, it is mentioned that he then showed his index and middle finger, pointing to the closeness of the two).” (Aluad) Though others related the hadith, Ahmad alone related it with the chain for this specific narration.

Through another chain, Imam Ahmad related from Anas ibn Maalik és that the Prophet # said, “I have been sent and the Hour like these two.” He 2% then pointed to his index and middle fingers. With its different chains, Bukhaaree, Muslim, Ahmad, and At-Tirmidhee related this hadith.

The Narration Of Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah

Jaabir ibn ‘Abdullah 4 said, “The Messenger of Allah 2% gave us a sermon. He praised Allah, extolling Allah will praises that are worthy of Him (i.e., of His Greatness and Majesty). He 3 then said, “As for what follows: the most truthful speech is the Book of Allah, and the best guidance is the guidance of Muhammad. The most evil of affairs are newly invented ones (i.e., in the religion), and every Bid’ah (innovation) is a misguidance.’ He then raised his voice and his cheeks reddened. His anger became severe when he 2 mentioned the Hour, as if he was the warner of an army. He # then said, “The Hour has come to you. | was sent and the Hour like this.’ He then indicated with his index and middle fingers...” (Ahmad) Muslim, An-Nasaaee, and Ibn Maajah related this hadith through different chains.

The Narration Of Sahl Ibn Sa’ad #&

Sahl w& said, “I saw the Prophet # make a sign with the two fingers that follow the thumb: the index and middle fingers, and he was saying, ‘I have been sent and the Hour like this.” (Muslit)

190 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

The Narration Of Abu Hurairah

In another narration, Abu Hurairah 4 reported that the Prophet #4 said, “I have been sent and the Hour like these two.” (Bukhaaree) Ton Maajah related it through a different chain. In his narration, he mentioned, “And he joined his fingers together.”

Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa related this hadith from Abu Jabeevah ibn Ad-Dahhaak 4: “I have been sent in the Qasam of the Hour.” He said, “When the first part of it appears.” The chain of this narration is good, though the narration is not mentioned in books, nor did Ahmad Ibn Hanbal relate it.

The hadith -When Compared To The Time That Has Passed, The Day Of Resurrection Is Indeed Near

‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Umar -& related that he heard the Messenger of Allah 3G say as he was standing on the Minbar (pulpit), “Indeed, your remaining in comparison to those who came before you from the nations is like the time between the ‘Asr prayer and the setting of the sun. The People of the Torah were given the Torah, and they applied it until the middle of the day, and then they became incapable. They were given a Qeeraat (a measurement, indicating a great reward). Then the people of the Injeel (Bible) were given the Injeel, and they applied it until the ‘Asr Prayer; they were given Qeeraat Qeeraat. Then you were given the Quran, and you applied it until the setting of the sun, and you were given two Qeeraats, two Qeeraats. The People of the Torah and the Injeel said, ‘Our Lord, these are less in, work, but greater in reward.’ He $@ said, ‘Did 1 wrong you in anything regarding your wages” They said, ‘No.’ He # said, ‘Then that is My Favor, which I bestow upon whomsoever I please.” (Ahmad) Bukhaatee related it as well, but through a different chain,

Ibn ‘Umar 4 said, “The Messenger of Allah 2% said, ‘Indeed your period in comparison to the period of those nations who passed

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 191

before you is like the time between the ‘Asr prayer and the setting of the sum (when compared to the entire day). Your example and the example of the Jews and the Christians...” He then mentioned the hadith in its entirety. (Bukhaaree)

Another Chain From Ibn ‘Umar #%

Mujaahid related that Ibn ‘Umar a said, “We were seated with the Prophet 4% when the sun was over Qu’aiq’aan (a mountain beside Makkah Al-Mukarramah), after Al-‘Asr. He # said, “Your lives when compared to the lives of those who have passed is only like what remains from this day when compared to what has passed. from it.” (Ahmad) The chain of this narration is Hasan, good.”
content_id:
Gambaran Tentang Orang-Orang Yang Akan Hidup Di Masa Akhir Zaman

'Abdullah ibn 'Amr meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, "Kiamat tidak akan tiba hingga Allah mengangkat syariat-Nya dari penduduk bumi, dan al-'Ujjaajah (orang-orang awam dan massa) akan tertinggal di atasnya (yakni di bumi) — mereka tidak akan memerintahkan yang ma'ruf dan tidak pula melarang yang munkar." (Ahmad) Dan yang berikut ini diriwayatkan oleh 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr, yang menisbatkannya kepada Nabi: "Hingga Allah mengangkat syariat-Nya dari penduduk bumi."

Sesungguhnya Sebagian Bentuk Ucapan Adalah Sihir

'Abdullah ibn Mas'ood melaporkan bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah bersabda, "Sesungguhnya, sebagian bentuk ucapan adalah sihir. Dan (hanya) seburuk-buruk manusia yang akan hidup ketika Kiamat menimpa mereka, serta mereka yang menjadikan kuburan (yakni kuburan orang-orang dari kalangan mereka) sebagai Masaajid.'" (Ahmad) Sanad riwayat ini sahih. 'Abdullah berkata, "Rasulullah bersabda, 'Kiamat tidak akan datang kecuali kepada sejahat-jahat manusia.'" (Ahmad) Muslim meriwayatkan hadith yang sama melalui sanad yang berbeda.

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 187

Sesaat Sebelum Datangnya Kiamat Manusia Akan Kehilangan Sisi Kemanusiaannya

Kami telah menyebutkan hadith berikut ini: "Kaum laki-laki akan sedikit, dan kaum perempuan akan sangat banyak, sedemikian rupa sehingga untuk setiap 50 perempuan, akan ada satu wali (laki-laki) tunggal, yang akan mereka jadikan tempat berlindung. Dan mereka akan melakukan perbuatan keji di jalanan sebagaimana binatang melakukannya." Karena kami telah menyebutkan berbagai sanad dan redaksi riwayat ini, kami tidak akan mengulanginya di sini. Dan segala puji hanya bagi Allah.

Kiamat Tidak Akan Turun Kepada Orang Yang Bertauhid

Anas melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, "Kiamat tidak akan tiba hingga tidak lagi diucapkan di bumi, Laa ilaaha illallaah (tidak ada yang berhak diibadahi selain Allah)." (Ahmad) Muslim meriwayatkan hadith yang sama, melalui perawi yang berbeda, dengan redaksi berikut: "Kiamat tidak akan tiba hingga tidak lagi diucapkan di bumi, Allah, Allah."

Anas melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, "Kiamat tidak akan tiba kepada orang yang mengucapkan, 'Allah, Allah.'" (Ahmad) Muslim meriwayatkan hal yang sama, namun melalui sanad yang berbeda.

Anas meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda, "Kiamat tidak akan tiba hingga tidak lagi diucapkan di bumi, 'Allah, Allah.'" (Ahmad) Sanad ini memenuhi syarat-syarat Bukhari dan Muslim. At-Tirmidzi berkata tentang sanadnya untuk riwayat ini, "Hasan." At-Tirmidzi kemudian meriwayatkannya sebagai perkataan Anas sendiri, lalu berkata, "Ini lebih sahih dari yang pertama."

Kiamat Hanya Akan Turun Kepada Mereka Yang Tidak Memerintahkan Yang Ma'ruf Dan Tidak Melarang Yang Munkar

Hal ini ditunjukkan oleh sabda Nabi, "Hingga tidak lagi diucapkan di bumi, 'Allah, Allah.'"

Ada dua pendapat mengenai hal ini:

188 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

tidak akan memerintahkan yang ma'ruf dan tidak pula melarang yang munkar."

2) Allah tidak akan disebutkan di bumi, dan nama-Nya pun tidak akan dikenal di atasnya. Dan itu terjadi pada suatu zaman yang paling rusak — ketika manusia telah kehilangan sisi kemanusiaannya, suatu masa ketika kekufuran, kefasikan, dan kemaksiatan kepada Allah tersebar luas. Makna ini ditunjukkan dalam hadith lain: "Kiamat tidak akan tiba hingga tidak lagi diucapkan di bumi, Laa ilaaha illallaah (Tidak ada yang berhak diibadahi selain Allah)."

Sejahat-Jahat Manusia Hidup Ketika Kiamat Tiba

Kami telah menyebutkan hadith berikut ini: "Sesungguhnya orang tua akan berkata, 'Kami pernah ada ketika orang-orang di masa lalu mengucapkan: Tidak ada yang berhak diibadahi selain Allah.' Kemudian perkara itu semakin memburuk, dan keadaan semakin parah, hingga penyebutan Allah tidak lagi dilakukan di bumi: hal itu sepenuhnya terlupakan, dan tidak lagi dikenal di bumi. Mereka (yang hidup pada masa itu) adalah seburuk-buruk manusia, dan kepada merekalah Kiamat akan tiba." Dan dalam riwayat lain, Nabi bersabda, "Kiamat tidak akan tiba kecuali kepada sejahat-jahat manusia." Hal ini diungkapkan sedikit berbeda dalam riwayat berikut: "Sejahat-jahat manusia adalah mereka yang masih hidup ketika Kiamat menimpa mereka."

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 189

lemah, hingga Kiamat tiba kepada mereka."

Berbagai Sanad Untuk Hadith: "Aku Diutus Dan Kiamat Seperti Dua Ini"

Anas ibn Maalik pergi menemui Al-Waleed ibn 'Abdul-Malik, dan yang terakhir bertanya kepada yang pertama, "Sabda-sabda apa yang telah kamu dengar dari Rasulullah di mana beliau menyebutkan Kiamat?" Ia berkata, "Aku mendengar Rasulullah bersabda, 'Kamu dan Kiamat seperti dua ini (dalam riwayat lain disebutkan bahwa beliau kemudian menunjukkan jari telunjuk dan jari tengahnya, menunjukkan kedekatan keduanya).'" (Ahmad) Meskipun orang lain meriwayatkan hadith ini, Ahmad sendirilah yang meriwayatkannya dengan sanad untuk riwayat spesifik ini.

Melalui sanad lain, Imam Ahmad meriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Maalik bahwa Nabi bersabda, "Aku diutus dan Kiamat seperti dua ini." Beliau kemudian menunjuk pada jari telunjuk dan jari tengahnya. Dengan berbagai sanadnya, Bukhari, Muslim, Ahmad, dan At-Tirmidzi meriwayatkan hadith ini.

Riwayat Jaabir ibn 'Abdullah

Jaabir ibn 'Abdullah berkata, "Rasulullah menyampaikan khutbah kepada kami. Beliau memuji Allah, mengagungkan Allah dengan pujian-pujian yang layak bagi-Nya (yakni bagi Keagungan dan Kemuliaan-Nya). Beliau kemudian bersabda, 'Adapun yang berikut ini: ucapan yang paling benar adalah Kitab Allah, dan sebaik-baik petunjuk adalah petunjuk Muhammad. Seburuk-buruk perkara adalah yang baru-baru diada-adakan (yakni dalam agama), dan setiap bid'ah adalah kesesatan.' Kemudian beliau meninggikan suaranya dan kedua pipinya memerah. Kemarahannya menjadi sangat keras ketika beliau menyebutkan Kiamat, seolah-olah beliau adalah pemberi peringatan bagi sebuah pasukan. Beliau kemudian bersabda, 'Kiamat telah datang kepada kalian. Aku diutus dan Kiamat seperti ini.' Kemudian beliau mengisyaratkan dengan jari telunjuk dan jari tengahnya..." (Ahmad) Muslim, An-Nasaa'i, dan Ibn Maajah meriwayatkan hadith ini melalui sanad yang berbeda.

Riwayat Sahl ibn Sa'ad

Sahl berkata, "Aku melihat Nabi memberi isyarat dengan dua jari yang mengikuti ibu jari: jari telunjuk dan jari tengah, dan beliau bersabda, 'Aku diutus dan Kiamat seperti ini.'" (Muslim)

190 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Riwayat Abu Hurairah

Dalam riwayat lain, Abu Hurairah melaporkan bahwa Nabi bersabda, "Aku diutus dan Kiamat seperti dua ini." (Bukhari) Ibn Maajah meriwayatkannya melalui sanad yang berbeda. Dalam riwayatnya, ia menyebutkan, "Dan beliau menyatukan kedua jarinya."

Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa meriwayatkan hadith ini dari Abu Jabeerah ibn Ad-Dahhaak: "Aku diutus dalam Qasam Kiamat." Ia berkata, "Ketika bagian pertamanya muncul." Sanad riwayat ini baik, meskipun riwayat ini tidak disebutkan dalam kitab-kitab, dan Ahmad ibn Hanbal pun tidak meriwayatkannya.

Hadith — Dibandingkan Dengan Waktu Yang Telah Berlalu, Hari Kiamat Memang Sudah Dekat

'Abdullah ibn 'Umar meriwayatkan bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah bersabda ketika beliau berdiri di atas Minbar (mimbar), "Sesungguhnya sisa masa kalian dibandingkan dengan orang-orang yang datang sebelum kalian dari umat-umat terdahulu adalah seperti waktu antara salah Ashar dan terbenamnya matahari. Kaum Ahli Taurat diberikan Taurat, dan mereka mengamalkannya hingga pertengahan hari, kemudian mereka tidak mampu lagi. Mereka diberi satu Qeeraat (sebuah ukuran, menunjukkan pahala yang besar). Kemudian kaum Ahli Injil diberikan Injil, dan mereka mengamalkannya hingga salah Ashar; mereka diberi Qeeraat Qeeraat. Kemudian kalian diberikan Quran, dan kalian mengamalkannya hingga terbenamnya matahari, dan kalian diberi dua Qeeraat, dua Qeeraat. Kaum Ahli Taurat dan Injil berkata, 'Ya Tuhan kami, mereka lebih sedikit dalam amal, tetapi lebih besar dalam pahala.' Beliau bersabda, 'Apakah Aku menzalimi kalian dalam hal upah kalian?' Mereka berkata, 'Tidak.' Beliau bersabda, 'Maka itulah karunia-Ku, yang Aku berikan kepada siapa pun yang Aku kehendaki.'" (Ahmad) Bukhari pun meriwayatkannya, namun melalui sanad yang berbeda.

Ibn 'Umar berkata, "Rasulullah bersabda, 'Sesungguhnya masa kalian dibandingkan dengan masa umat-umat yang telah berlalu

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 191

sebelum kalian adalah seperti waktu antara salah Ashar dan terbenamnya matahari (dibandingkan dengan seluruh hari). Perumpamaan kalian dan perumpamaan kaum Yahudi dan Nasrani...'" Kemudian beliau menyebutkan hadith tersebut selengkapnya. (Bukhari)

Sanad Lain Dari Ibn 'Umar #%

Mujaahid meriwayatkan bahwa Ibn 'Umar berkata, "Kami sedang duduk bersama Nabi 4% ketika matahari berada di atas Qu'aiq'aan (sebuah gunung di dekat Makkah Al-Mukarramah), setelah Al-'Asr. Beliau # bersabda, "Umur kalian dibandingkan dengan umur orang-orang yang telah berlalu hanyalah seperti sisa hari ini dibandingkan dengan apa yang telah berlalu darinya." (Ahmad) Sanad riwayat ini adalah Hasan, baik."
content_ar: A Description Of The People Who Will Be Alive خلال The End Times ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr a related that الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, “The Hour will not arrive حتى الله takes His Shariah from the people of the earth, and the ‘Uijaajah (the mob and the common masses) will be left on it (Le. on earth) — they will neither promote الخير nor forbid الشر.” (أحمد) And the following was related by ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Amr -#e, who ascribed it to النبي ﷺ 2%: “حتى الله takes His Shariah from the people.” Indeed Some Forms Of Speech Are Magic ‘Abdullah ibn Mas‘ood < reported that he heard الرسول ﷺ of الله #% say, “Indeed, some forms of speech are magic. And (only) the worst of people will be alive عندما the Hour overtakes them and those who take their graves (i.e. the graves of people from them) as Masaajid.’” (أحمد) The chain of this narration is authentic. “Abdullah . said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله #¢ said, ‘The Hour will not come except upon the most الشر of people.” (A/mad) مسلم related the same الحديث through a different chain, الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 187 Just قبل The Coming Of The Hour People Will Become Bereft Of The Human Aspect We have already mentioned the following الحديث: “Men will be few, and women will be great in number, to the extent that for every 50 women, there will be a single (male) guardian, to whom they will resort to. And they will do lewd acts in the streets just as beasts do.” بما أن we have already mentioned the different chains and wordings of this narration, we will not do لذا here. And all praise is for الله. The Hour Will Not Descend Upon One Who Is An Islamic Monotheist أنس reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, “The Hour will not arrive حتى it is no longer said on earth, Laa ilagha illallaah (none has the الحق to be worshipped save الله).” (أحمد) مسلم related the same الحديث, through different narrators, and with the following wording: “The Hour will not arrive حتى it is no longer said on earth, الله, الله.” أنس # reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2g said, “The Hour will not arrive upon one who says, ‘الله, الله,” (أحمد) مسلم related the same, but through a different chain of narrators. أنس related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “The Hour will not arrive حتى it is no longer said on earth, ‘الله, الله.” (Alimad) This chain fulfills the conditions of both Bukhaaree and مسلم. AtTirmidhee said about his chain for this narration, “حسن.” At- ‘Tirmidhee then related it as a saying of أنس . and then said, “This is more authentic than the أولاً.” The Hour Will Only Descend Upon Those Who Neither Promote الخير Nor Forbid الشر This is indicated by the saying of النبي ﷺ #, “حتى it is no longer said on earth, ‘الله, الله.” ‘There are two opinions فيما يخص this: 188 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations will neither promote الخير nor forbid الشر.” 2) الله %@ will not be mentioned on earth, nor will His Name be known on it. And that is خلال an era which will be most corrupt — عندما human beings will become bereft of the human aspect in them, a time عندما there will be widespread disbelief, wickedness, and disobedience of الله 4. And this meaning is indicated in another الحديث: “The Hour will not arrive حتى it is no longer said on earth, Laailaaha illallaah (None has the الحق to be worshipped but الله).” The Most الشر Of People Are Alive عندما The Hour Comes We already mentioned the following الحديث: “Indeed the old man will say, ‘We were around عندما people in the past would say: None has the الحق to be worshipped but الله.’ Then the matter will intensify, and the situation will worsen, حتى الله’s mention will no longer be made on earth: it will be entirely forgotten, and it will not be known on earth, Those (who are around at that time) are the worst of people, and upon them the Hour will arrive.” And in another narration, النبي ﷺ #% said, “The Hour will not arrive except upon the most wicked of people.” This is expressed slightly differently in the following narration: “The most الشر of people are those who will be alive عندما the Hour overtakes them.” الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 189 weak, حتى the Hour arrives upon them.” The Different Chains For The الحديث: “] Have Been Sent And The Hour Like These Two” ‘أنس ibn Maalik .& went to Al-Waleed ibn ‘Abdul-مالك, and the latter asked of the former, “What sayings have you heard from الرسول ﷺ of الله #% in which he #& mentioned the Hour?” قال, “I heard الرسول ﷺ of الله 48 say, ‘You and the Hour are like these two (in other narrations, ورد that he then showed his index and middle finger, pointing to the closeness of the two).” (Aluad) Though others related the الحديث, أحمد alone related it with the chain for this specific narration. Through another chain, الإمام أحمد related from أنس ibn Maalik és that النبي ﷺ # said, “I have been sent and the Hour like these two.” He 2% then pointed to his index and middle fingers. With its different chains, Bukhaaree, مسلم, أحمد, and At-Tirmidhee related this الحديث. The Narration Of Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah Jaabir ibn ‘Abdullah 4 said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% gave us a sermon. He praised الله, extolling الله will praises that are worthy of Him (i.e., of His Greatness and Majesty). He 3 then said, “أما what follows: the most truthful speech is the الكتاب of الله, and the best الهداية is the الهداية of محمد ﷺ. The most الشر of affairs are newly invented ones (i.e., in the religion), and every Bid’ah (البدعة) is a misguidance.’ He then raised his voice and his cheeks reddened. His الغضب became severe عندما he 2 mentioned the Hour, as إذا he was the warner of an army. He # then said, “The Hour has come to you. | was sent and the Hour like this.’ He then indicated with his index and middle fingers...” (أحمد) مسلم, An-Nasaaee, and Ibn Maajah related this الحديث through different chains. The Narration Of Sahl Ibn Sa’ad #& Sahl w& said, “I saw النبي ﷺ # make a sign with the two fingers that follow the thumb: the index and middle fingers, and he was saying, ‘I have been sent and the Hour like this.” (Muslit) 190 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations The Narration Of أبو هريرة In another narration, أبو هريرة 4 reported that النبي ﷺ #4 said, “I have been sent and the Hour like these two.” (Bukhaaree) Ton Maajah related it through a different chain. In his narration, ذكر, “And he joined his fingers together.” أبو بكر ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa related this الحديث from Abu Jabeevah ibn Ad-Dahhaak 4: “I have been sent in the Qasam of the Hour.” قال, “عندما the أولاً part of it appears.” The chain of this narration is الخير, though the narration is not mentioned in الكتب, nor did أحمد ابن حنبل relate it. The الحديث -عندما Compared To The Time That Has Passed, The Day Of البعث Is Indeed Near ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘عمر -& related that he heard الرسول ﷺ of الله 3G say as he was standing on the Minbar (pulpit), “Indeed, your remaining in comparison to those who came قبل you from the nations is like the time between the ‘Asr الصلاة and the setting of the sun. The People of the Torah were given the Torah, and they applied it حتى the middle of the day, and then they became incapable. They were given a Qeeraat (a measurement, indicating a great الأجر). Then the people of the Injeel (Bible) were given the Injeel, and they applied it حتى the ‘Asr الصلاة; they were given Qeeraat Qeeraat. Then you were given the القرآن الكريم, and you applied it حتى the setting of the sun, and you were given two Qeeraats, two Qeeraats. The People of the Torah and the Injeel said, ‘Our Lord, these are less in, work, but greater in الأجر.’ He $@ said, ‘Did 1 الخطأ you in anything فيما يخص your wages” قالوا, ‘No.’ He # said, ‘Then that is My النعمة, which I bestow upon whomsoever I please.” (أحمد) Bukhaatee related it as well, but through a different chain, Ibn ‘عمر 4 said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, ‘Indeed your period in comparison to the period of those nations who passed الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 191 قبل you is like the time between the ‘Asr الصلاة and the setting of the sum (عندما compared to the entire day). Your example and the example of the Jews and the Christians...” He then mentioned the الحديث in its entirety. (Bukhaaree) Another Chain From Ibn ‘عمر #% Mujaahid related that Ibn ‘عمر a said, “We were seated with النبي ﷺ 4% عندما the sun was over Qu’aiq’aan (a mountain beside مكة المكرمة Al-Mukarramah), بعد Al-‘Asr. He # said, “Your lives عندما compared to the lives of those who have passed is only like what remains from this day عندما compared to what has passed. from it.” (أحمد) The chain of this narration is حسن, الخير.”
